name: Embalse Arenoso y Ademuz — circular desde Pedro Abad
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Pedro Abad offer diverse terrain across the province of Córdoba, Spain. The area features mostly paved surfaces, suitable for various skill levels, with routes characterized by a mix of flatter sections and climbs. Cyclists can explore landscapes that include water bodies like the Navallana Reservoir and the Rio Guadalquivir, providing varied scenery. The region's topography allows for both moderate rides and more challenging ascents. Best road cycling routes around Pedro Abad The most popular road cycling route is Embalse Arenoso y Ademuz — circular desde Pedro Abad, a 31.6 miles (50.9 km) trail that takes 2 hours 24 minutes to complete. This moderate route offers a loop around Embalse Arenoso. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Puente Mocho – Los Cansinos Straight Road loop from Pedro Abad, a moderate 51.4 miles (82.8 km) path. This route features a notable straight road section. Local road cyclists also love the Villanueva de Córdoba – Bell Tower of Adamuz loop from Pedro Abad, a 77.3 miles (124.4 km) trail leading through the countryside towards the Bell Tower of Adamuz, often completed in about 6 hours 9 minutes. Road cycling around Pedro Abad is defined by varied terrain, including sections along the Rio Guadalquivir, views of the Navallana Reservoir, and a mix of flatter roads and significant climbs. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate to difficult. The routes in Pedro Abad are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.2 stars from over 10 reviews. More than 1,400 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Pedro Abad's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Pedro Abad?
A: There are over 55 road cycling routes around Pedro Abad, offering diverse terrain for cyclists. These routes range from easy to difficult, ensuring options for various skill levels.

Q: What types of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes near Pedro Abad?
A: The road cycling routes around Pedro Abad feature mostly paved surfaces with a mix of flatter sections and climbs. You'll encounter varied landscapes, including views of water bodies like the Navallana Reservoir and sections along the Rio Guadalquivir.

Q: Are there any easy road cycling routes suitable for beginners in Pedro Abad?
A: Yes, Pedro Abad offers 5 easy road cycling routes. While specific names for easy routes are not provided in detail, the area is known for having options suitable for those new to road cycling or looking for a more relaxed ride.

Q: What are some challenging road cycling routes for experienced cyclists?
A: For experienced cyclists seeking a challenge, Pedro Abad offers 19 difficult routes. A notable option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20126322" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Villanueva de Córdoba – Bell Tower of Adamuz loop from Pedro Abad&lt;/a&gt;, a 124.4 km trail with significant elevation gain. Another demanding ride is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40305386" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Marmolejo Spa – Yeguas Reservoir loop from Pedro Abad&lt;/a&gt;, covering 116 km.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see along the road cycling routes?
A: Many routes offer scenic views of the Navallana Reservoir and sections along the Rio Guadalquivir. You might also pass by points of interest such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5358670" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Villafranca Iron Bridge&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6443221" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fountain in Montoro&lt;/a&gt;. The settlement of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5442042" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Montoro&lt;/a&gt; itself is also a highlight.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes starting from Pedro Abad?
A: Yes, many routes are circular. For example, the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1017160460/embalse-arenoso-y-ademuz-circular-desde-pedro-abad" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Embalse Arenoso y Ademuz — circular desde Pedro Abad&lt;/a&gt; is a 50.9 km loop. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40344392" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puente Mocho – Los Cansinos Straight Road loop from Pedro Abad&lt;/a&gt;, which is an 82.8 km circular path.

Q: What do other road cyclists say about the routes in Pedro Abad?
A: The road cycling routes in Pedro Abad are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.2 stars from over 10 reviews. Cyclists often praise the varied terrain, which includes both flatter roads and significant climbs, and the scenic views of the region's natural features.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Pedro Abad?
A: While specific seasonal data isn't provided, the region's climate suggests that spring and autumn would likely offer the most comfortable temperatures for road cycling. Summers can be very hot in Andalusia, and winters are generally mild but can have cooler, wetter periods.

Q: Are there any road cycling routes that pass through towns or villages where I can find cafes or restaurants?
A: Yes, some routes will take you through or near local settlements. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40344443" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Montoro – Marmolejo Spa loop from Pedro Abad&lt;/a&gt; passes through Montoro, where you can find amenities like cafes and restaurants. Exploring routes that connect towns will generally offer more opportunities for refreshment stops.

Q: Is there parking available for cyclists at the start of routes in Pedro Abad?
A: While specific parking facilities for cyclists are not detailed, Pedro Abad is a town, and general parking options would typically be available within the municipality or at the start of popular routes. It's advisable to check local parking regulations upon arrival.

Q: Can I combine road cycling with cultural exploration in the Pedro Abad area?
A: Absolutely. Pedro Abad is located in the broader region of Córdoba, which is rich in historical and cultural sites. While cycling, you can enjoy the natural landscapes, and then easily access nearby attractions like the historic city of Córdoba, allowing for a blend of outdoor activity and cultural discovery.
tips: Reservoir fed by the river of the same name, which regulates its waters before reaching the Guadalquivir.
The El Salto dam is a hydroelectric infrastructure located on the Guadalquivir river, in the municipality of El Carpio

The most characteristic point of Pedro Abad is the parish church of Nuestra Señora de la Asunción from the 14th century. The town located in the meander of the Guadalquivir river was formerly very important in river traffic within this area of great agricultural potential.

waypoints.name: Church of Our Lady of the Assumption of Pedro Abad
